channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: API Errors
item.description: May 23, 19:41 UTC Investigating - We are investigating elevated error rates for API requests. Customers may experience failures when making API calls.

item.title: Cloudflare Zero Trust Gateway Issues — FedRAMP High
item.description: May 23, 19:37 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 23, 19:35 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 23, 19:29 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. May 23, 19:25 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: Intermittent errors with app installation token authentication
item.description: May 23, 19:32 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. May 23, 19:32 UTC Update - This is fully mitigated, we will continue to monitor to ensure...

item.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: GitHub has rolled out new controls for npm to improve the security of the software supply chain, giving maintainers the ability to explicitly approve a release prior to the packages becoming publicly available for installation. Called staged publishing, the feature is now generally available on...

item.title: Claude Mythos AI Finds 10,000 High-Severity Flaws in Widely Used Software
item.description: Anthropic on Friday disclosed that Project Glasswing has helped uncover more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across some of the most "systemically" important software across the world since the cybersecurity initiative went live last month. Project Glasswing is an effort led...

item.title: ‘Underminr’ Vulnerability Lets Attackers Hide Malicious Connections Behind Trusted Domains
item.description: The stealthy vulnerability impacts roughly 88 million domains and can be exploited to bypass DNS filtering and hide command-and-control traffic. The post ‘Underminr’ Vulnerability Lets Attackers Hide Malicious Connections Behind Trusted Domains appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Laravel-Lang PHP Packages Compromised to Deliver Cross-Platform Credential Stealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has targeted multiple PHP packages belonging to Laravel-Lang to deliver a comprehensive credential-stealing framework. The affected packages include - laravel-lang/lang laravel-lang/http-statuses...

item.title: LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin CVE-2026-48172 Exploited to Run Scripts as Root
item.description: A maximum-severity security vulnerability impacting LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin has come under active exploitation in the wild. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-48172 (CVSS score: 10.0), relates to an instance of incorrect privilege assignment that an attacker could abuse to run arbitrary scripts...

item.title: Drupal Core SQL Injection Bug Actively Exploited, Added to CISA KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a recently patched critical security flaw impacting Drupal Core to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-9082 (CVSS score:...

item.title: [AINews] All Model Labs are now Agent Labs
item.description: a quiet day lets us tie together a few quotes as all model labs become agent labs

item.title: Benchmarking and Improving Monitors for Out-Of-Distribution Alignment Failure in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2605.21602v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many safety and alignment failures of large language models (LLMs) occur due to out-of-distribution (OOD) situations: unusual prompt or response patterns that are unforeseen by model developers. We systematically study whether LLM monitoring pipelines...

item.title: TO-Agents: A Multi-Agent AI Pipeline for Preference-Guided Topology Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.21622v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Topology optimization can generate efficient structures, but designers often must manually translate qualitative intent, such as desired visual style, product experience, or manufacturability into solver settings that are not directly tied to those...

item.title: The Shape of Testimony: A Scalable Framework for Oral History Archive Comparison
item.description: arXiv:2605.21623v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Researchers in Holocaust studies have often distinguished between two styles of oral survivor testimony: the USC Shoah Foundation's interviews tend to follow a structured, interviewer-guided format, whereas the Yale Fortunoff Video Archive generally...

item.title: MindLoom: Composing Thought Modes for Frontier-Level Reasoning Data Synthesis
item.description: arXiv:2605.21630v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although LLMs have made substantial progress in reasoning, systematically producing frontier-level reasoning data remains difficult. Existing synthesis methods often have limited visibility into the structural factors that govern problem difficulty,...

item.title: AOP-Wiki EMOD 3.0: Data Model Expansions and Content Evaluation Framework for Using Agentic AI to Improve Integration between AOPs and New Approach Methodologies (NAMs)
item.description: arXiv:2605.21645v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Adverse Outcome Pathways (AOP) are logic models that causally link biological mechanisms that can be measured in a lab to adverse outcomes, relevant to chemical regulatory endpoints. AOPs contextualize new approach methodologies (NAMs), in vitro and...

item.title: Temporal Contrastive Transformer for Financial Crime Detection: Self-Supervised Sequence Embeddings via Predictive Contrastive Coding
item.description: arXiv:2605.21490v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the Temporal Contrastive Transformer (TCT), a representation learning framework designed to capture contextual temporal dynamics in sequences of financial transactions. The model is trained using a self-supervised contrastive objective to...

item.title: Teaching Language Models to Forecast Research Success Through Comparative Idea Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2605.21491v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As language models accelerate scientific research by automating hypothesis generation and implementation, a new bottleneck emerges: evaluating and filtering hundreds of AI-generated ideas without exhaustive experimentation. We ask whether LMs can...

item.title: The Attribution Impossibility: No Feature Ranking Is Faithful, Stable, and Complete Under Collinearity
item.description: arXiv:2605.21492v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: No feature ranking can be simultaneously faithful, stable, and complete when features are collinear. For collinear pairs, ranking reduces to a coin flip. We prove this impossibility, quantify it for four model classes, resolve it via ensemble...

item.title: Don't Collapse Your Features: Why CenterLoss Hurts OOD Detection and Multi-Scale Mahalanobis Wins
item.description: arXiv:2605.21493v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The ability to detect out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs is fundamental to safe deployment of machine learning systems. Yet, current methods often rely on feature representations that are optimised solely for classification accuracy, neglecting the...

item.title: Double descent for least-squares interpolation on contaminated data: A simulation study
item.description: arXiv:2605.21494v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Overparametrized models can exhibit an excellent generalization performance, although they should be prone to overfitting according to classical statistical theory. The discovery of the "double descent", indicating that the generalization error...

item.title: Towards Speed-of-Light Text Generation with Nemotron-Labs Diffusion Language Models
Worker Pages Post-Deployment Errors
item.description: May 22, 20:55 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Customers experiencing 5xx errors with Pages deployments should redeploy to resolve. May 22, 20:49 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been released customer experiencing 5xx errors on their pages deployments should redeploy to resolve. May 22,...

item.title: Audit Log Delays
item.description: May 22, 17:33 UTC Identified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. May 22, 16:55 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare Audit Log processing is running behind, causing a delay in timely delivery of audit data. This impacts v2 audit logs. v1 is unaffected.

item.title: Drupal Vulnerability in Hacker Crosshairs Shortly After Disclosure
item.description: Drupal is warning users that it has already seen attempts to exploit CVE-2026-9082 and security firms are seeing attacks against thousands of websites. The post Drupal Vulnerability in Hacker Crosshairs Shortly After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Increase in users hitting Codex rate limits
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components Codex Web (Degraded performance) CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance) Codex API (Degraded performance)

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub...

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: May 22, 16:33 UTC Resolved - Impacted APIs and the Cloudflare dashboard have fully recovered. May 22, 16:22 UTC Update - We have rolled back the affected service and are seeing impacted APIs and our dashboard begin to recover.We expect recovery to be gradual as in-memory caches (not our CDN caches)...

item.title: Specialization Beats Scale: A Strategic Variable Most AI Procurement Decisions Overlook
In Other News: Industrial Router Exploitation, CISA KEV Nomination Form, Gas Station Hacking
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: CISA contractor exposes credentials, Mythos testing and new features, Huawei router flaw triggered telecom blackout. The post In Other News: Industrial Router Exploitation, CISA KEV Nomination Form, Gas Station Hacking appeared first...

item.title: Canadian Man Arrested for Operating Kimwolf Botnet
item.description: Jacob Butler, 23, has been arrested in Canada and US authorities are seeking his extradition on computer hacking charges. The post Canadian Man Arrested for Operating Kimwolf Botnet appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: coding’s future, the ‘Steroid Olympics,’ and AI-driven science
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not At Anthropic’s developer event in London this week, Code with...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 22, 10:31 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 22, 10:26 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 22, 10:17 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Zhipu AI Launches GLM-5.1 High-Speed API: 400 Tokens/s Sets New Global Benchmark
item.description: Zhipu AI has launched GLM-5.1-highspeed, an API variant of its GLM-5.1 model delivering 400 tokens per second — reportedly the fastest inference speed among major global LLM providers.

item.title: Google I/O showed how the path for AI-driven science is shifting
item.description: During Tuesday’s Google I/O keynote, Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, proclaimed that we are currently “standing in the foothills of the singularity.” It was a striking statement—the singularity is the theoretical future moment when AI rapidly exceeds human intelligence and dramatically...

item.title: HiDream AI Unveils Unified Multimodal Model with 200B+ Parameters, Raises New Funding
item.description: HiDream AI has unveiled HiDream-O1-Image-Pro, a native unified multimodal model with over 200 billion parameters, alongside announcing a new billion-level funding round.

item.title: ByteDance Releases Lance, a Lightweight Native Unified Multimodal AI Model
item.description: ByteDance has released Lance, an open-source native unified multimodal AI model with only 3 billion activated parameters, designed to handle image understanding, generation, and video tasks in a single system.

item.title: CapCut Partners with Google Gemini to Bring AI Video Editing Directly to the Gemini App
item.description: ByteDance's CapCut has announced a partnership with Google Gemini, enabling users to access CapCut's video editing tools directly within the Gemini app for AI-assisted content creation.

item.title: Anker Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro Launches with Self-Developed AI Audio Chip, Sets Guinness Record
item.description: Anker has unveiled the Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro and Pro Max wireless earbuds at its first-ever product launch event, featuring a proprietary AI audio chip that has earned a Guinness World Record.

item.title: ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested
item.description: The FBI says First VPN has been used by dozens of ransomware groups for network reconnaissance and intrusions. The post ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Enhanced Games fit right in with the rest of 2026’s longevity vibes
item.description: This Sunday, a group of 42 athletes will gather in Las Vegas to compete in a somewhat unusual sporting competition. Participants in the inaugural Enhanced Games are being encouraged to take performance-enhancing drugs. The goal is to “push the boundaries of human performance.” The games’ organizers...

item.title: Elevated error rate on multiple models
item.description: May 22, 06:32 UTCUpdate - We are monitoring recovery on Claude Opus 4.7, and working to fully resolve remaining errors impacting Haiku 4.5. We will provide additional updates shortly.May 22, 06:17 UTCUpdate - We are currently working to resolve some additional errors impacting Claude Opus 4.7 and...

item.title: [AINews] New AI Infra unicorns: Exa, Modal, TurboPuffer
item.description: a quiet day lets us feature fundraises!

item.title: CR4T: Rewrite-Based Guardrails for Adolescent LLM Safety
item.description: arXiv:2605.21609v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly embedded in adolescent digital environments, mediating information seeking, advice, and emotionally sensitive interactions. Yet existing safety mechanisms remain largely grounded in adult-centric norms and...

item.title: Broadening Access to Transportation Safety Data with Generative AI: A Schema-Grounded Framework for Spatial Natural Language Queries
item.description: arXiv:2605.21712v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transportation safety analysis requires integrating crash records, roadway attributes, and geospatial data through GIS-based workflows, but access remains uneven across agencies and community stakeholders. Technical prerequisites create a gap between...

item.title: Sem-Detect: Semantic Level Detection of AI Generated Peer-Reviews
item.description: arXiv:2605.21713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How can we distinguish whether a peer review was written by a human or generated by an AI model? We argue that, in this setting, authorship should not be attributed solely from the textual features of a review, but also from the ideas, judgments, and...

item.title: Probabilistic Attribution For Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.21726v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The generative nature of Large Language Models (LLMs) is reflected in the conditional probabilities they compute to sample each response token given the previous tokens. These probabilities encode the distributional structure that the model learns in...

item.title: RankJudge: A Multi-Turn LLM-as-a-Judge Synthetic Benchmark Generator
item.description: arXiv:2605.21748v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As interactive LLM-based applications are created and refined, model developers need to evaluate the quality of generated text along many possible axes. For simpler systems, human evaluation may be practical, but in complicated systems like...

item.title: Elevated latency and error rates for ChatGPT 5.5 Thinking
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
item.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months....

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai
item.description: May 21, 21:08 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 21, 20:42 UTCMonitoring - We have applied a fix and success rates for requests on Claude.ai have returned to normal. We are monitoring closely to ensure no further issues.May 21, 20:24 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating elevated...
